summary: sqlxl is a SQL Server <-> Excel bridge. Three core flows: template-driven DML (insert/update/import), query export (export), and schema inference (infer). Extensibility via BulkOpFeature rows in the target database.

override_flags.name: --connection
override_flags.description: Ad-hoc connection string. Overrides --profile and the active profile.

override_flags.name: --profile
override_flags.description: Named saved profile. Overrides the active profile.

commands.name: init
commands.summary: Connect to a SQL Server database, install SqlXL infrastructure, and save the connection profile.

flags.name: --connection
flags.description: SQL Server connection string for the database to initialize.

flags.name: --profile
flags.description: Name to save this connection under.

behaviors: Installs the SqlXl.* schema (BulkOpFeatures, ColumnUIConfigurations, DebugLog, Meta_Columns, RequestContext, SavedQueries) into the target database.
Idempotent: re-running updates the profile and re-applies infrastructure.

positional_args.name: PROFILE
positional_args.description: Name of the profile to activate.

commands.name: insert
commands.summary: Generate an INSERT template, or import a filled template into a table.

flags.name: --table
flags.description: Domain table, e.g. dbo.Products or just Products (assumes dbo).

flags.name: --file
flags.description: Excel file to import. Omit to generate a template.

flags.name: --output
flags.description: Output path for generated template. Default: <Table>_insert_YYYYMMDD.xlsx.

flags.name: --no-launch
flags.description: Do not auto-open the generated Excel file. USE THIS in agents/scripts.

flags.name: --connection
flags.description: Override profile.

flags.name: --profile
flags.description: Override active profile.

behaviors: If no BulkOpFeature exists for the table, AUTO-SCAFFOLDS one: creates staging table SqlXl.Staging_<Table>_ForInserts, sproc <Table>_InsertFromStaging, and a BulkOpFeatures row of type 'Insert'.
Generated templates exclude IDENTITY/PK columns from the Data sheet (they remain in the Metadata sheet with IsPrimaryKey=YES).
Templates are 3-sheet workbooks: Data, DropdownOptions, Metadata.

examples.comment: Generate template; auto-scaffolds feature on first run.

commands.name: update
commands.summary: Generate an UPDATE template pre-populated with existing rows, or import a filled template.

flags.name: --table
flags.description: Domain table.

flags.name: --where
flags.description: SQL WHERE clause to filter rows for the template (template generation only — IGNORED on import).

flags.name: --file
flags.description: Excel file to import. Omit to generate.

flags.name: --output
flags.description: Default: <Table>_update_YYYYMMDD.xlsx.

flags.name: --no-launch
flags.description: Do not auto-open Excel.

behaviors: Template is pre-populated with existing rows matching --where (or all rows if omitted).
Import is keyed by primary key — PK columns must be present and unchanged in the imported file.

commands.name: import
commands.summary: Generate a template or import data via a custom BulkOpFeature.

flags.name: --feature
flags.description: BulkOpFeature ID from SqlXl.BulkOpFeatures.

flags.name: --file
flags.description: Excel file to import.

flags.name: --output
flags.description: Default: Feature_YYYYMMDD.xlsx.

flags.name: --no-launch
flags.description: Do not auto-open Excel.

behaviors: Generic entry point for any BulkOpFeature (Insert, Update, Delete, or Custom).
Use this when invoking a manually-authored Custom feature, or when targeting a specific feature ID.

commands.name: export
commands.summary: Export SQL query results to Excel.

flags.name: --query
flags.description: SELECT statement (validated via SqlXl infrastructure before execution).

flags.name: --output
flags.description: Default: export_YYYYMMDD_HHmmss.xlsx.

flags.name: --no-launch
flags.description: Do not auto-open Excel.

behaviors: Single-sheet workbook output: column headers in row 1, data below.
Query is validated against SqlXl infrastructure before execution — `init` must have been run on the target DB.

commands.name: test
commands.summary: Auto-generate and run test data against all configured features for a table.

flags.name: --table
flags.description: Domain table to test.

flags.name: --rows
flags.description: Test rows per feature. Max: 100.

behaviors: Synthesizes rows respecting unique constraints both against existing data AND across the generated batch.
Test data is COMMITTED to the database — run only against disposable DBs.
Each configured feature runs independently; one feature failing does not stop others.
With small tables and narrow unique-column value spaces, --rows N (N>1) frequently collides. Use --rows 1 to verify pipeline.

commands.name: infer
commands.summary: Read an Excel file and emit a CREATE TABLE statement inferred from the data.

positional_args.name: INPUT
positional_args.description: Path to the .xlsx file to infer schema from.

flags.name: --table
flags.description: Target table name. Default: sanitized file basename.

flags.name: --schema
flags.description: Target schema name.

flags.name: --sheet
flags.description: Worksheet to read (REQUIRED when workbook has more than one sheet).

flags.name: --sample-size
flags.description: Number of data rows to sample.

flags.name: --confidence-threshold
flags.description: Min valid-ratio for a type to be selected.

flags.name: --mode
flags.description: permissive (invalid -> NULL) or strict (any invalid forces NVARCHAR fallback).

flags.name: --max-varchar
flags.description: Cap for inferred NVARCHAR length.

flags.name: --date-format
flags.description: us (M/d/yyyy + ISO + long forms) or iso (ISO + long forms only).

flags.name: --output
flags.description: Write generated DDL to a file. Default: stdout.

flags.name: --report
flags.description: Write a JSON inference report to this path.

behaviors: Pure read operation — no DB connection needed.
DDL goes to stdout unless --output is given (pipeable to sqlcmd).

commands.name: demo
commands.summary: Create the SqlXlDemo database with sample data (drops and recreates).

flags.name: --connection
flags.description: SQL Server connection string for the server that will host SqlXlDemo.

flags.name: --yes
flags.description: Skip the confirmation prompt.

behaviors: Drops and recreates the SqlXlDemo database — all existing data lost.
Installs SqlXL infrastructure and seeds 11 domain tables plus 1 pre-configured Custom feature ('Assign User Roles').

sheets.name: DropdownOptions
sheets.purpose: FK / lookup values. One row per allowable option per FK column.

sheets.name: Metadata
sheets.purpose: Per-column schema info for the import side.

bulk_op_feature_schema.name: GetRowsToEdit_SelectStatement
bulk_op_feature_schema.description: Defines the Data sheet shape; supports [Col|Alias] aliasing syntax.

bulk_op_feature_schema.name: SprocToProcessPerfectStagedData
bulk_op_feature_schema.description: Sproc that promotes validated staged rows into the domain table.

agent_best_practices.rule: Always pass --no-launch on commands that produce .xlsx
agent_best_practices.rationale: Without it, Excel opens AND locks the file, breaking subsequent re-imports.

agent_best_practices.rule: Ensure the file is closed in Excel before --file imports
agent_best_practices.rationale: The tool errors out on locked files.

agent_best_practices.rule: Parse the `File: <path>` line from stdout to capture output paths
agent_best_practices.rationale: Use the absolute path printed by the tool, not your guess of cwd.
The interactive confirmation prompt blocks non-TTY callers.
Diagnostics (including detailed validation errors) are on stderr.

workflows.name: round_trip_insert
workflows.title: Round-trip insert (auto-scaffolded)
workflows.description: Insert new rows into a table that has no pre-configured BulkOpFeature. The first call auto-scaffolds the feature.

steps.comment: Generate template; auto-scaffolds feature on first run.

steps.instruction: Edit t.xlsx and add rows to the Data sheet. For FK columns, use values from DropdownOptions in `<id> - <label>` format. Close Excel.
Edit u.xlsx values in place; keep PK columns unchanged.
Fill rows per the feature's column shape.

workflows.name: infer_and_init
workflows.title: Infer DDL from spreadsheet, build the table, then init

steps.command: sqlxl infer customers.xlsx --schema sales --table Customers --output ddl.sql

gotchas: `update --where` applies only to template generation, not import. Import is keyed by PK.
`init` is required before `export` — query validation depends on SqlXl infrastructure.
`infer` writes DDL to stdout by default; pass --output to write a file.
`test` commits data to the DB — never run against production.
`test --rows N>1` often collides on unique constraints in small tables; not a tool bug.
Auto-scaffolding (on first `insert` for a table) creates DB objects as a side-effect — note this when running against shared databases.
The `[Col|Alias]` aliasing syntax in GetRowsToEdit_SelectStatement is sqlxl-specific, not standard SQL.

builtin_schema.name: BulkOpFeatures
builtin_schema.purpose: Feature registry — one row per declarative bulk-op definition.

builtin_schema.name: ColumnUIConfigurations
builtin_schema.purpose: Per-column UI hints (display formatting, dropdown sources).

builtin_schema.name: RequestContext
builtin_schema.purpose: Per-request execution context, used during imports.

builtin_schema.name: DebugLog
builtin_schema.purpose: Diagnostic log written by sprocs during processing.
